experimental_design_for_optimization.purpose: To prepare and optimize gastroenteric floating combination tablets (GRCTs) containing 500 mg amoxicillin trihydrate (AMX) and 125 mg levofloxacin (LVX) for sustained release and stability at gastric pH for eradicating resistant H. pylori.
experimental_design_for_optimization.preparation_method: Direct compression method using a manual hydraulic press.

equations.density_calculation: D = W / V, where D = density, W = weight, V = volume
equations.peak_area_percentage: peak area of each active ingredient × 100 / total sum of all related peak areas

LVX_HPLC_conditions.column: C18 (5 μm, 4.6 mm × 15 cm; Osaka Soda)
LVX_HPLC_conditions.mobile_phase: 0.87% pentanesulfonic acid in phosphate buffer (pH 2.4) and acetonitrile (8:2, v/v)

pre_compression_parameters.note: The article does not provide pre-compression parameters such as mass distribution, friability, or size with standard deviation for individual batches. These parameters were evaluated post-compression.
pre_compression_parameters.general_observation: Mean weight of all prepared GRCTs ranged between 997.47 and 998.47 mg/tablet, with a relative standard deviation of < 0.2%.

variables.variable: Effervescent agent concentration (citric acid + sodium bicarbonate)

fourier_transform_infrared_spectral_meas.note: FTIR spectral measurements are not mentioned or discussed in this article.

process_parameters.compression_device: Manual hydraulic press (SUG-10; SMART HYDRAULICS CO., LTD, Siheung-si, Republic of Korea)
process_parameters.compression_pressure: Varied: 30 bar (041_F1-041_F8 standard), 40 bar, 50 bar (for optimization)
process_parameters.dwelling_time: Standard 5 s; also tested at 2 s for optimization
equipment_used: Drug dissolution tester (708-DS, Agilent Technologies Inc.)
HPLC system (2695 HPLC, Waters) with UV-vis detector (Waters 2487)

process_conditions.pH_medium: pH 4.0 acetate buffer (0.05 M) for drug release and floating tests

release_properties.factors_influencing_release: pH 4.0, HPMC (4000 cP) + CMC, effervescent agents (5%)
pH 4.0, HPMC (4000 cP) + CMC, effervescent agents (10%)
pH 4.0, excessive effervescent agents (15%), rapid initial burst, disintegration by 6h
release_properties.release_kinetics: Could not be determined due to large initial burst release and disintegration

tablet_properties.lag_time_minutes: <5 s (at 30 bar), increased to 47 min at 50 bar/5s

tablet_properties.stability: Stable at pH >4.0 (AMX peak area 96.77% at 12h)

experimental_data.statistical_analysis: Data presented as mean ± standard deviation (n=3). Coefficients of determination (r²) used to assess fit to kinetic models.
